funding_agency: Connecticut Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services (DMHAS) — Problem Gambling Services
funding_chain: DMHAS Problem Gambling Services → Regional Behavioral Health Action Organizations (RBHAOs) + Connecticut Council on Problem Gambling (CCPG) → Gambling Awareness Connecticut portal
blurb: Gambling Awareness Connecticut is the statewide umbrella web portal coordinating Connecticut's five-region prevention infrastructure under DMHAS Problem Gambling Services. The site aggregates prevention resources, the College Gambling Prevention Initiative, the Youth Media Project, multicultural outreach, fact sheets, and the regional contact directory for the RBHAO-led prevention workforce. It serves as the public-facing entry point for DMHAS-funded community awareness, training, and the statewide Gambling Education Resource Van operated with MCCA. Audiences include the general public, youth, college students, and historically underserved populations served by the multicultural initiative.

funding_agency: Connecticut Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services (DMHAS) — Problem Gambling Services
funding_chain: DMHAS Problem Gambling Services → ThinkArgus (creative vendor) → Gambling Awareness Connecticut + Responsible Play CT landing pages
blurb: Know the Risks launched in June 2024 as a DMHAS-funded multicultural awareness campaign developed with ThinkArgus, a marketing firm specializing in cultural diversity and problem-gambling messaging. The campaign was built to reach historically underserved communities and special populations at greater risk for gambling-related harm. Assets include billboards, bus tails, a web landing page at responsibleplayct.org/risks, a digital toolkit, social media content, brochures, posters, flyers, and outreach materials available in English and Spanish. Free print materials are distributed through the CT Clearinghouse.

funding_agency: Connecticut Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services (DMHAS) — Problem Gambling Services
funding_chain: DMHAS Problem Gambling Services + Connecticut Council on Problem Gambling (CCPG) + Connecticut Lottery Corporation + licensed operators → Responsible Play CT portal
blurb: Responsible Play the CT Way is the consumer-facing responsible-gambling portal jointly maintained by DMHAS Problem Gambling Services, CCPG, and Connecticut's gambling-industry stakeholders including the Connecticut Lottery. The site provides risk-awareness content, self-exclusion pathways, and a request form for the statewide Gambling Education Resource Van. It anchors the seasonal sports-betting awareness pushes coordinated with the CT Department of Consumer Protection during March Madness and the NFL season.

funding_agency: Delaware Division of Substance Abuse and Mental Health (DSAMH)
funding_chain: DSAMH → Delaware Council on Gambling Problems (DCGP, NCPG affiliate) → Aloysius Butler & Clark (creative vendor)
blurb: Dangerous Levels Ahead is DCGP's youth-and-parent prevention campaign focused on the video gaming-to-gambling pipeline (loot boxes, in-game purchases, simulated gambling). Developed with Wilmington-based Aloysius Butler & Clark and funded through DCGP's DSAMH contract, the campaign supports two paired sites — for youth and for adults — with downloadable posters, brochures for schools, and an online risk quiz. The campaign responds to Delaware Youth Risk Behavior Survey findings that nearly 44% of middle and high school students gambled in the past year while 66% of parents had never discussed gambling with their children. Materials are available in English and Spanish.

campaign_name: Don't Let Mobile Sports Betting Get in the Way
funding_agency: Delaware Division of Substance Abuse and Mental Health (DSAMH)
funding_chain: DSAMH → Delaware Council on Gambling Problems (DCGP) → Aloysius Butler & Clark (creative vendor)
blurb: This DCGP campaign targets mobile sports betting risk for Delaware youth and young adults, paired with a parents companion page. Citing University of Delaware Center for Drug and Alcohol Studies data tying youth sports betting to a 50% increased risk of vaping, marijuana, alcohol, and other risky behaviors, the site explains parlays, prop bets, live in-game wagering, and the addiction trajectory. Calls to action route to the DCGP Helpline at 1-888-850-8888. Funded through DCGP's DSAMH contract; creative by Aloysius Butler & Clark.

campaign_name: ADEPT Maine Problem Gambling Services / PG4ME
funding_agency: Maine Department of Health and Human Services — Maine CDC (Gambling Addiction Prevention and Treatment Fund, 5 M.R.S. §20006-B)
funding_chain: Maine DHHS / Maine CDC → ADEPT Educational Institute of Maine (state contractor) → community mini-grant subrecipients (PG4ME)
blurb: ADEPT Maine is the state-contracted problem-gambling prevention and workforce arm funded through Maine's Gambling Addiction Prevention and Treatment Fund. ADEPT coordinates Maine's Annual Conference on Problem Gambling Awareness, the PG4ME Mini-Grant initiative awarding up to $5,000 grants to community projects advancing awareness and prevention, the Maine Problem Gambling Screening Toolkit, the Gambling Addiction Treatment Reimbursement Network, and the statewide self-exclusion site network. The PG4ME branding anchors the public-awareness side. Maine residents reach services through 211 Maine, which routes 207-area-code calls to 1-800-GAMBLER.

campaign_name: Maryland Center of Excellence on Problem Gambling Public Awareness Campaigns
funding_agency: Maryland Department of Health — Behavioral Health Administration (BHA), funded via the Problem Gambling Fund (State Government §9-1A-33)
funding_chain: Maryland DOH BHA → Maryland Center of Excellence on Problem Gambling at University of Maryland School of Medicine (state contractor) → Maryland Alliance for Responsible Gambling (MARG, public-private partnership including MLGCA)
blurb: The Maryland Center of Excellence on Problem Gambling, housed at the University of Maryland School of Medicine and funded by the Maryland Department of Health's Behavioral Health Administration through the Problem Gambling Fund, runs the state's continuous public-awareness program. Active 2025-2026 campaigns include the Gift Responsibly / Too Young to Bet holiday campaign (with MLGCA participation in the national Gift Responsibly Campaign), Problem Gambling Awareness Month / National Problem Gambling Screening Day activities, and the Pause the betting… ENJOY the Sport sports-betting message line. Free materials, posters, and toolkits are distributed at no cost to organizations statewide. Treatment is coordinated through the Maryland Alliance for Responsible Gambling (mdgamblinghelp.org) and 1-800-GAMBLER.

funding_agency: Massachusetts Gaming Commission (MGC) — Public Health Trust Fund
funding_chain: MGC (Public Health Trust Fund, 5% statutory PG dedication) → Massachusetts Council on Gaming and Health (MACGH, state contractor) → PlayWell Hubs at casino properties + playwell.org
ncpg_awards: Predecessor GameSense received NCPG Social Responsibility Award (2015) and WLA Best Overall Responsible Gambling Program (2010); PlayWell rebrand launched March 2026
blurb: PlayWell is the Massachusetts Gaming Commission's wholly owned responsible-gaming and player-health program, replacing GameSense in March 2026. PlayWell Hubs operate inside Plainridge Park Casino, MGM Springfield, and Encore Boston Harbor with trained PlayWell Advisors providing on-property education, voluntary self-exclusion enrollment, and PlayMyWay budgeting-tool support. The PlayWell trademark is held by the MGC and program administration is contracted to the Massachusetts Council on Gaming and Health. Resources include the Cost of Play Calculator, Truth or Myth Quiz, Lower Risk Gambling Guidelines, sport-specific game guides, and a veterans landing page. Massachusetts is the only U.S. jurisdiction statutorily required to provide on-property responsible-gaming resources.

funding_agency: Massachusetts Gaming Commission (MGC) + Massachusetts Attorney General's Office
funding_chain: MGC + AG's Office → Massachusetts Council on Gaming and Health (MACGH, state contractor) → Youth Sports Betting Safety Coalition (NCAA, Red Sox, Patriots, Revolution, Celtics, Bruins, Civic Action Project) → high schools and community-based orgs
blurb: Launched May 2025 by the Massachusetts AG's Office, the Massachusetts Gaming Commission, and MACGH together with the Youth Sports Betting Safety Coalition (including the five Boston-area pro sports franchises and the NCAA), this is the first U.S. youth gambling-harm prevention curriculum focused specifically on sports betting and daily fantasy sports. The five-session program teaches risk recognition, gambling-myth literacy, media literacy, and financial literacy through lived-experience storytelling. Phase 1 reached 440 youth across multiple Massachusetts high schools and community-based organizations; the curriculum targets ages 12-20.

No state-agency-funded named public-awareness campaign was identified for New Hampshire as of May 2026. NH problem-gambling treatment funding flows through the DHHS Bureau of Drug and Alcohol Services and the Problem Gambling Fund (RSA 287-I), and the New Hampshire Council on Problem Gambling (NCPG affiliate) operates the helpline at 603-724-1605, but there is no named external public-awareness campaign meeting the inclusion criteria. The NH Council for Responsible Gaming launched April 2026 as an industry-and-stakeholder body but is too new to have a state-funded public-awareness campaign of record.

funding_agency: New Jersey Department of Human Services — Division of Mental Health and Addiction Services (DMHAS); Compulsive Gambling Treatment Fund (N.J.S.A. 5:12-189)
funding_chain: NJ DHS DMHAS (Compulsive Gambling Treatment Fund) + NJ Lottery contract → Council on Compulsive Gambling of New Jersey (CCGNJ, NCPG affiliate) → 1-800-GAMBLER helpline + 800gambler.org
ncpg_awards: CCGNJ owns the 1-800-GAMBLER mark; September 2025 NJ court ruling affirmed exclusive rights
blurb: The Council on Compulsive Gambling of New Jersey operates the 1-800-GAMBLER helpline and the 800gambler.org public-awareness portal under contract with the NJ Division of Mental Health and Addiction Services and in partnership with the NJ Lottery. The Lottery prints 1-800-GAMBLER on all tickets, brochures, and signage and tags it on all radio and TV advertising. 2025 awareness theming — The Hidden Epidemic: A Public Health Concern — was carried jointly with NJ Lottery for Problem Gambling Awareness Month. Helpline volume has grown nearly fourfold since 2018, with majority of callers under 34. The Council also runs the Not 18 Yet? No Bet underage-gambling awareness campaign with the NJ Lottery.

funding_agency: New Jersey Lottery (under N.J.S.A. 5:9) + New Jersey DHS DMHAS
funding_chain: NJ Lottery + DMHAS Compulsive Gambling Treatment Fund → Council on Compulsive Gambling of New Jersey (CCGNJ) → annual joint awareness campaign
blurb: Not 18 Yet? No Bet is the New Jersey Lottery's longstanding annual underage-gambling awareness campaign run jointly with CCGNJ. The campaign discourages adults from gifting lottery products to minors and reinforces the legal-age messaging required by state law. Annual press materials are co-issued by the NJ Lottery and CCGNJ, with content amplified through retailer signage, point-of-sale messaging, and CCGNJ's social channels.

funding_agency: New York State Office of Addiction Services and Supports (OASAS)
funding_chain: OASAS (1% VLT/casino GGR + 1% mobile sports-betting GGR dedicated funding) → New York Council on Problem Gambling (NYCPG, NCPG affiliate) → KnowTheOdds.org
blurb: KnowTheOdds is NYCPG's statewide problem-gambling information hub, funded by OASAS. The site provides ebooks (Understanding Problem Gambling, The Hidden Addiction, Staying Safe after Problem Gambling, The Faces of Problem Gambling), interactive infographics (The Path of Problem Gambling, The Cost of Gambling), PSAs, and a support directory pointing to NYS treatment and self-help resources. The campaign anchors NYCPG's general-public awareness arm and is referenced by the OASAS HOPEline (1-877-846-7369). KnowTheOdds is the long-running flagship of New York's mature dedicated-revenue problem-gambling funding model.

funding_agency: New York State Office of Addiction Services and Supports (OASAS)
funding_chain: OASAS (Problem Gambling Resource Centers + 1% dedicated GGR funding) → New York Council on Problem Gambling (NYCPG) → YouDecideNY.org
blurb: YOU(th) Decide NY is NYCPG's OASAS-funded underage and young-adult gambling prevention project. The site provides tailored landing pages for youth (12-17), young adults (under 25), parents, and community influencers, with palm cards, brochures, posters, the Talk to Your Teen video series (sports gambling, loot boxes, charity gambling), and underage-gambling research summaries. The project leverages OASAS's seven regional Problem Gambling Resource Centers (Western NY, Finger Lakes, Central NY, Capital, Mid-Hudson, NYC, Long Island) for local delivery.

funding_agency: New York State Gaming Commission — Division of the Lottery
funding_chain: NY Gaming Commission Division of the Lottery → Responsible Play Partnership (NY Gaming Commission + OASAS + NYCPG) → PSA campaign distribution statewide
ncpg_awards: Three 2024 NASPL Batchy Awards (responsible gaming category) — North American Association of State and Provincial Lotteries
blurb: Small Risk/Big Gamble is the New York Gaming Commission Lottery Division's ongoing responsible-gaming PSA campaign, distributed statewide through the Responsible Play Partnership coordinated with OASAS and NYCPG. The campaign earned three of the four 2024 NASPL Batchy responsible-gaming awards (corporate-sector recognition for North American lotteries). Joint Responsible Play Partnership activities also include the national Gift Responsibly Campaign each holiday season warning against gifting lottery tickets to minors.

funding_agency: Pennsylvania Gaming Control Board (PGCB)
funding_chain: PGCB → in-house campaign + Council on Compulsive Gambling of Pennsylvania (CCGP, NCPG affiliate, DDAP contractor) endorsement → social media + dedicated site + free downloadable resources for educators and counselors
blurb: What's Really at Stake is the Pennsylvania Gaming Control Board's underage-gambling awareness campaign, launched March 2026 to coincide with Problem Gambling Awareness Month and the NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament. The tagline — Underage gambling will cost you — anchors social-media content, online advertising, a dedicated site with a six-question risk check quiz, and free downloadable posters, table tents, and social-media graphics for schools, universities, and counselors. The campaign cites data that 75% of U.S. college students gambled in the past year and that 6% of college students meet criteria for a serious gambling problem (roughly double the adult U.S. average).

funding_agency: Pennsylvania Gaming Control Board (PGCB) — Office of Compulsive and Problem Gambling
funding_chain: PGCB Office of Compulsive and Problem Gambling → ResponsiblePlay.pa.gov portal + statewide self-exclusion program
blurb: ResponsiblePlay.pa.gov is PGCB's dedicated consumer-facing responsible-gambling and self-exclusion portal. It hosts the Do I Have a Gambling Problem screener, gambling-addiction help resources, the statewide self-exclusion enrollment pathway (casino, online, fantasy contests, VGTs), and the 1-800-GAMBLER helpline integration (call/chat/text). DDAP-funded treatment access flows through the CCGP-operated helpline.

funding_agency: Rhode Island Department of Behavioral Healthcare, Developmental Disabilities and Hospitals (BHDDH) — Problem Gambling Services
funding_chain: BHDDH Problem Gambling Services → Rhode Island Council on Problem Gambling (RICPG) + RI Lottery + Bally's Corporation + EPIC Global Solutions co-sponsorship → Game Changer Challenge competition
ncpg_awards: Inaugural 2025 Nancy Murray Award given internally; no NCPG award documented
blurb: The RI Game Changer Challenge is an annual creative-PSA contest for Rhode Island college students ages 18-25, run by the Rhode Island Council on Problem Gambling with Problem Gambling Services (BHDDH), the RI Lottery, Bally's, and EPIC Global Solutions as listed sponsors. Students submit 15-second TikTok/Instagram Reels or 30-second PSAs on responsible-gaming and gambling-harm-prevention themes. The 2025 award introduced the Nancy Murray Award honoring the long-serving RI Lottery Problem Gambling Program Manager, with a $3,000 tuition prize to the winning college submission. Note: campaign funding mixes state (BHDDH PGS, RI Lottery) and operator (Bally's) sources; state-agency funding is verifiable.

funding_agency: Vermont Department of Mental Health (DMH); funded under Act 63 of 2023 sports-wagering revenue allocation
funding_chain: Vermont DMH (problem-gambling appropriation) ← Vermont Department of Liquor and Lottery (sports-betting revenue) → Institute for Consumer Health (state contractor managing site) → VTGamblingHelp.org
blurb: VTGamblingHelp.org is Vermont's statewide problem-gambling information and help portal, launched July 2024 and explicitly funded by the Vermont Department of Mental Health through revenue allocations from the Department of Liquor and Lottery under Act 63 of 2023. The site is managed by the Institute for Consumer Health and offers a 24/7 live-chat function, a routing to 1-800-GAMBLER, a sports-betting guide, a self-supports section, dedicated landing pages for providers and parents, and the Vermont Provider Free Training Program offering up to 30 free CEUs. Vermont DMH estimates 11,600+ Vermonters struggle with problem gambling; lawmakers allocated $500,000 for 2025 PG programming. Vermont also participates in the national Gift Responsibly campaign through the VT Lottery.

No state-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaign identified for Alabama as of May 2026. Alabama has no state lottery, no commercial casinos, no state-regulated sports betting, and no dedicated state appropriation for problem-gambling response. The Alabama Department of Mental Health (mh.alabama.gov) operates a general 24/7 addiction helpline but does not run a named gambling-specific public-awareness campaign. The Alabama Council on Compulsive Gambling (alccg.org) is a private 501(c)(3) nonprofit operating without verifiable state-agency funding; problem-gambling response in Alabama defaults to the national 1-800-GAMBLER line.

No state-agency-funded named gambling-related public-awareness campaign identified for Arkansas as of May 2026. The Arkansas Department of Human Services Division of Aging, Adult, and Behavioral Health Services (DAABHS) administers gambling treatment funding but has not launched a verifiable named public-awareness campaign with its own URL. The Arkansas Problem Gambling Council (arpgcouncil.org), founded 2021, is a private nonprofit that routes callers to 1-800-GAMBLER and runs prevention and counselor-training programs; the Council advocated for Act 798 of 2025 (Digital Self-Exclusion Act) but the public-awareness work is privately funded rather than via traceable state-agency contract. The Arkansas Scholarship Lottery participates annually in the NCPG Gift Responsibly Campaign but does not operate a standalone state public-awareness campaign brand.

funding_agency: Florida Council on Compulsive Gambling (FCCG, nonprofit) historically funded by Florida Lottery responsible-gaming dedication and Seminole Tribal contributions; FGCC oversight role
funding_chain: FCCG (NCPG state affiliate, 501(c)(3)) → historically Florida Lottery responsible-gaming dedication 1988-2024 → Seminole Tribe of Florida continued RG support → Florida Gaming Control Commission Compulsive or Addictive Gambling Prevention Program funding 2007-June 2024 (program lapsed and FGCC re-let to Kindbridge/PlayWise 2025)
blurb: The 888-ADMIT-IT campaign is Florida's flagship problem-gambling helpline awareness brand, operated by the Florida Council on Compulsive Gambling (FCCG). It promotes a 24/7 confidential and multilingual HelpLine (call or text 888-ADMIT-IT / 1-888-236-4848), live chat at gamblinghelp.org, and a mobile app, with services in English, Spanish, and Haitian Creole. The campaign covers Problem Gambling Awareness Month (March, aligned with NCAA March Madness), Responsible Gaming Education Month, and the Gift Responsibly holiday campaign, plus year-round outreach to college students, military members and veterans, seniors, and adolescents. Note that FCCG declined to bid on the Florida Gaming Control Commission's 2024 RFP that previously funded its statutory prevention work; the Commission allowed the Compulsive or Addictive Gambling Prevention Program to lapse July 1, 2024 and has since contracted Kindbridge Research Institute to operate a separate 1-833-PLAYWISE helpline. FCCG continues to operate 888-ADMIT-IT under Seminole compact and historic state-affiliated funding.

funding_agency: Georgia Council on Problem Gambling (NCPG state affiliate); Georgia Lottery Corporation funding to Georgia Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities (DBHDD)
funding_chain: Georgia Lottery Corporation (~$400,000/year contribution) → Georgia DBHDD Addictive Disease Branch → Georgia Council on Problem Gambling (NCPG state affiliate) → Georgia Gambling Help (georgiagamblinghelp.org)
blurb: The Georgia Gambling Help website (georgiagamblinghelp.org) is the public-facing brand of the Georgia Council on Problem Gambling (GCPG), serving as Georgia's central state-affiliated problem-gambling outreach, public-awareness, training, and clinician-referral hub. It provides recovery roadmap content, a clinician/counselor directory, family resources, and media outreach for Problem Gambling Awareness Month around March Madness. Funding flows from the Georgia Lottery Corporation's roughly $400,000 annual contribution to the Georgia Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities (DBHDD) Addictive Disease Branch, which partners with the Council on awareness and education work. Helpline routing uses 1-800-GAMBLER and the Georgia Crisis and Access Line (1-800-715-4225); the Georgia Lottery also participates each December in the NCPG Gift Responsibly Campaign at Level 2.

funding_agency: Kentucky Council on Problem Gambling (KYCPG, NCPG state affiliate); Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services, Department for Behavioral Health, Developmental and Intellectual Disabilities (DBHDID) via Problem Gambling Assistance Account
funding_chain: Kentucky Sports Wagering Act 2023 (HB 551) → Problem Gambling Assistance Account → Kentucky CHFS DBHDID Problem Gambling Program → KYCPG (NCPG state affiliate) operating kygamblinghelp.org and outreach
blurb: Kentucky Gambling Help (kygamblinghelp.org) is the public-awareness, self-assessment, and counselor-locator site of the Kentucky Council on Problem Gambling (KYCPG), the NCPG state affiliate. It directs users to 1-800-GAMBLER for crisis intervention and referral, hosts the KYCPG annual conference, runs the "Whether, When, How Much?" responsible-gambling messaging, and supports outreach in schools and community settings. The Kentucky Cabinet for Health and Family Services Department for Behavioral Health, Developmental and Intellectual Disabilities (DBHDID) houses the formal state Problem Gambling Program, which is funded through the Problem Gambling Assistance Account created by the 2023 Sports Wagering Act (HB 551) and expanded by 2026 HB 904. DBHDID directs Kentuckians to kygamblinghelp.org, 1-800-GAMBLER, and Gamblers Anonymous. Helpline contacts roughly quadrupled 2022-2024 following sports-betting launch, and KYCPG outreach scaled accordingly.

funding_agency: Louisiana Department of Health, Office of Behavioral Health (LDH OBH) Compulsive and Problem Gaming Program; Louisiana Association on Compulsive Gambling (LACG) operates helpline and CORE residential treatment
funding_chain: Louisiana R.S. 27.1 Uniform Compulsive and Problem Gambling Program → Louisiana Lottery Corporation first $500,000 annual proceeds dedication → Louisiana Office of Behavioral Health (LDH) → Louisiana Association on Compulsive Gambling (LACG, NCPG state affiliate) operating helpforgambling.org and Center of Recovery (CORE) residential program
blurb: The 1-877-770-STOP Louisiana Problem Gamblers Helpline campaign is Louisiana's flagship state-funded gambling awareness and treatment-routing brand, operated by the Louisiana Association on Compulsive Gambling (LACG) at helpforgambling.org. The 24/7 confidential helpline (1-877-770-STOP / 7867) has handled over 2.2 million contacts since 2000 and connects callers to the Center of Recovery (CORE) — one of the first state-supported residential gambling-addiction programs in the U.S., located in Shreveport — and the LACG Intensive Outpatient Program. Funding flows from the Louisiana Lottery Corporation's first $500,000 annual proceeds dedication and from gaming revenue dedicated to the LDH Office of Behavioral Health Compulsive and Problem Gaming Program under La. R.S. 27.1. The LACG is also one of two certified 988 Lifeline centers in Louisiana.

funding_agency: Louisiana Department of Justice, Gaming Division, in partnership with Louisiana Department of Health, Office of Behavioral Health
funding_chain: Louisiana Department of Justice Gaming Division + Louisiana Department of Health Office of Behavioral Health → Problem Gambling Resource Services program → freegamblinghelpla.org public-facing site
blurb: Problem Gambling Resource Services (PGRS, freegamblinghelpla.org) is a Louisiana program led jointly by the Louisiana Department of Justice Gaming Division and the Louisiana Department of Health Office of Behavioral Health. The program brings awareness to the legal community — attorneys, judges, probation officers, court professionals — about problem gambling and the free state-funded gambling treatment services available to all Louisiana residents. PGRS conducts in-person presentations on request, distributes brochures and educational materials, and routes Louisianans to the 1-877-770-STOP helpline and to the LDH-funded specialized gambling treatment provider network. The campaign is unusual in the Southeast for being explicitly co-branded by a state Attorney General Gaming Division alongside the state behavioral health agency.

campaign_name: Mississippi Council on Problem and Compulsive Gambling (MCPCG) Public Awareness
funding_agency: Mississippi Department of Mental Health (DMH) under Miss. Code Ann. § 41-4-1 et seq.; Mississippi Council on Problem and Compulsive Gambling (NCPG state affiliate)
funding_chain: Mississippi Department of Mental Health (state appropriation under § 41-4-1 et seq.) → Mississippi Council on Problem and Compulsive Gambling (NCPG state affiliate, EIN 64-0851101) → 1-888-777-9696 helpline + msgambler.org awareness, training, and prevention programs
blurb: The Mississippi Council on Problem and Compulsive Gambling (MCPCG, msgambler.org) is Mississippi's state-affiliated problem-gambling public-awareness, training, and helpline hub. The Council operates a 24/7 confidential helpline at 1-888-777-9696 (text "msgambler" to 53342), publishes the state Gamblers Anonymous schedule, runs a self-assessment tool, and delivers statewide training and education to social-service agencies, community organizations, and healthcare providers. The mission explicitly includes developing education, awareness, and prevention programs for adolescents, senior citizens, and other groups impacted by problem gambling. State-agency funding flows from the Mississippi Department of Mental Health under Miss. Code Ann. § 41-4-1 et seq., supplemented by Mississippi Gaming Commission and Mississippi Lottery Corporation contributions; the MCPCG is the NCPG state affiliate.

campaign_name: More Than A Game (NC Problem Gambling Program)
funding_agency: North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services (NCDHHS) Division of Mental Health, Developmental Disabilities, and Substance Use Services
funding_chain: North Carolina Education Lottery Act dedication (N.C. Gen. Stat. § 18C-163) + Problem Gambling Education and Treatment Fund (S.L. 2023-42, N.C. Gen. Stat. § 18C-1014, ~$2M annual sports-wagering dedication) → NCDHHS Division of MH/DD/SUS → NC Problem Gambling Program → morethanagame.nc.gov
blurb: More Than A Game (morethanagame.nc.gov) is the umbrella state-agency campaign of the North Carolina Department of Health and Human Services Division of Mental Health, Developmental Disabilities, and Substance Use Services. It directs North Carolinians to free help — call 1-877-718-5543, text "morethanagamenc" to 53342, or chat online — and offers up to 12 sessions of no-cost counseling with licensed clinicians, the RecoverMe self-guided mobile app, NODS self-assessment, recovery-coach connections, and clinician training. It also houses the "Stacked Deck" youth prevention initiative, the only SAMHSA-recognized evidence-based gambling-prevention curriculum for teens, available via $5,000 mini-grants to middle and high schools and Boys & Girls Clubs; Stacked Deck reduced reported youth gambling activity from 41% to 36% in 2022 evaluations. Funding flows from the NC Lottery dedication under § 18C-163 and the Problem Gambling Education and Treatment Fund created by the 2023 sports-wagering statute (~$2 million annual dedication).

funding_agency: South Carolina Education Lottery Commission, in partnership with South Carolina Office of Substance Use Services (formerly DAODAS) of the SC Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Disabilities
funding_chain: South Carolina Education Lottery Act (S.C. Code Title 59 ch. 150) → SC Education Lottery Commission Play Responsibly dedication → BetterU education program co-promoted with BHDD/OSUS (formerly DAODAS) Gambling Addiction Services → SC Gambling Helpline 1-877-452-5155
blurb: Better U (sceducationlottery.com/BetterU) is the South Carolina Education Lottery's responsible-play education initiative, structured as a "Lottery 101" classroom. It teaches the math and odds behind Lottery games, busts common Lottery myths, lays out warning signs of problem gambling, and routes players to the South Carolina Gambling Helpline at 1-877-452-5155 (a toll-free, confidential, Lottery-funded line managed by the BHDD Office of Substance Use Services, the former DAODAS). The campaign is the South Carolina state-government-aligned vehicle for problem-gambling awareness in a jurisdiction with no commercial casinos, no sports betting, and a narrow legal-gambling footprint; OSUS operates statewide treatment through 46 county offices, with Lottery dedication funding gambling-specific clinical services. The SC Education Lottery is NASPL/NCPG Responsible Gambling Verified.

funding_agency: Tennessee Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services (TDMHSAS); program operated by Tennessee Association of Alcohol, Drug and other Addiction Services (TAADAS)
funding_chain: Tennessee Sports Gaming Act 2019 (1.85% handle tax with carve-out) → TDMHSAS Problem Gambling funding → TAADAS Problem Gambling Outreach, Education, and Referrals Program → TN REDLINE (1-800-889-9789) + provider database
blurb: The TN REDLINE Problem Gambling Outreach program is Tennessee's primary state-funded problem-gambling awareness vehicle, funded by a grant contract with the Tennessee Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services (TDMHSAS) and operated by the Tennessee Association of Alcohol, Drug and other Addiction Services (TAADAS). It is a statewide, multi-purpose program that raises public awareness through public service announcements, media news releases, and brochure distribution, maintains the searchable Gambling Recovery Database of providers, and routes callers and texters to the 24/7 TN REDLINE at 1-800-889-9789 for information and referrals on alcohol, drug, problem-gambling, and other addiction concerns. TDMHSAS funds problem-gambling treatment from a statutory carve-out of the state's unique 1.85%-of-handle sports-wagering tax created by the 2019 Tennessee Sports Gaming Act.

funding_agency: Virginia Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Services (DBHDS); developed with Omni Institute and Community Service Boards (CSBs)
funding_chain: Code of Virginia Title 58.1 ch. 40 sports-wagering and casino tax carve-out → DBHDS Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund (PGTSF) → Beyond the Bet campaign (DBHDS + Omni Institute + CSB prevention workgroup) → mandatory dissemination by CSBs receiving problem-gambling funds
blurb: Beyond the Bet (launched July 2025) is Virginia's flagship DBHDS-funded sports-betting prevention and awareness campaign, created by the Department of Behavioral Health and Developmental Services with the Omni Institute and a workgroup of prevention staff from Community Service Boards across the state. The campaign targets young adults — including college students and early-career professionals — and the general public, using non-stigmatizing, asset-based messaging like "It's a Game, Not a Paycheck," "Don't Let Bets Become Debts," "Big Risks ≠ Big Rewards," and "Bet on Your Team Self/Future." A complete bilingual (English/Spanish) toolkit at the DBHDS Problem Gambling Support page provides billboards, internal and external bus boards, flyers, brochures, rack cards, wallet cards, radio scripts, animated graphics, and social media assets. Dissemination is required for any CSB receiving Problem Gambling Treatment and Support Fund dollars, and the campaign is co-branded with the Virginia Council on Problem Gambling helpline 1-888-532-3500 and the national 1-800-GAMBLER.

campaign_name: 1-800-GAMBLER West Virginia / Problem Gamblers Help Network
funding_agency: West Virginia Department of Health and Human Resources (DHHR); West Virginia Lottery Problem Gambling Treatment Fund; operated by First Choice Services
funding_chain: West Virginia Lottery Problem Gambling Treatment Fund (statutory carve-out from racetrack VLT, sports wagering, and iGaming revenue) → West Virginia DHHR Problem Gambling funding → First Choice Services (Charleston, WV) operating Problem Gamblers Help Network → 1800gambler.net awareness, prevention grantees, and 24/7 helpline
blurb: West Virginia's Problem Gamblers Help Network — branded as 1-800-GAMBLER WV at 1800gambler.net — is one of the longest continuously operating dedicated state problem-gambling helplines in the U.S., dating to 2000. Operated by Charleston-based nonprofit First Choice Services and funded through the West Virginia DHHR Problem Gambling Treatment Fund (a statutory carve-out from West Virginia Lottery racino VLT, sports wagering, and iGaming revenue), the 24/7 helpline (1-800-426-2537) connected about 1,500 West Virginians to gambling-addiction help last year and partnered with CHESS Health in 2025 to launch the Connections recovery app customized for problem gambling. The website hosts symptoms guidance, a Problem Gambling Severity Index self-quiz, youth and college prevention content ("Get Ahead of the Game"), training for clinicians, a network of prevention grantees, and recovery-event listings. The Network turned 25 in September 2025.

funding_agency: Illinois Department of Human Services, Division of Substance Use Prevention and Recovery
funding_chain: IDHS/SUPR (Statewide Compulsive Gambling Program) → Health Resources in Action (HRiA) contractor → Are You Really Winning? campaign
blurb: "Are You Really Winning?" is the Illinois Department of Human Services' statewide problem-gambling public-awareness initiative, launched in 2022 and refreshed annually for Problem Gambling Awareness Month. The campaign runs across TV, radio, social media, and community sponsorships to surface the signs and symptoms of gambling disorder, normalize help-seeking, and drive traffic to the 1-800-GAMBLER helpline and a text line (GAMB to 833234). It is coordinated under the IDHS Division of Substance Use Prevention and Recovery's Statewide Compulsive Gambling Program in partnership with Health Resources in Action, whose statewide needs assessment estimated roughly 400,000 Illinois adults meet criteria for gambling disorder and another 700,000 are at risk.

funding_agency: Indiana Family and Social Services Administration, Division of Mental Health and Addiction (DMHA)
funding_chain: FSSA/DMHA (Indiana Problem Gamblers' Assistance Fund) → contract to Indiana University Prevention Insights → IPGAP
blurb: IPGAP is Indiana's state-contracted prevention, public-awareness, and workforce-development program for problem gambling, housed at Indiana University's Prevention Insights and funded through a DMHA contract drawn from the Indiana Problem Gamblers' Assistance Fund. It maintains the statewide problem-gambling referral line (800-994-8448), distributes Problem Gambling Awareness Month toolkits, runs a clinician and prevention-specialist listserv, and develops curricula for K-12, college, and community settings. IPGAP coordinates closely with the Indiana Gaming Commission's voluntary exclusion outreach and with the NCPG-affiliate Indiana Council on Problem Gambling.

funding_agency: Iowa Department of Health and Human Services, Office of Problem Gambling
funding_chain: Iowa HHS Office of Problem Gambling → contract to Your Life Iowa (operated by Iowa HHS with vendor support) → 1-800-BETS OFF helpline and statewide media
blurb: 1-800-BETS OFF is Iowa's longest-running gambling-disorder public-awareness brand, originally launched in the 1990s and consolidated under the Your Life Iowa portal in October 2017 alongside the state's suicide, substance-use, and mental-health helplines. The Iowa HHS Office of Problem Gambling funds statewide TV, radio, digital, and casino-floor advertising that drives Iowans to the 24/7 confidential helpline and the yourlifeiowa.org/gambling resource hub, which carries self-screens, treatment locators, voluntary self-exclusion enrollment, and family supports. State surveys have consistently shown roughly 88% unaided awareness of the 1-800-BETS OFF brand, among the highest helpline recognition rates in the country.

funding_agency: Kansas Department for Aging and Disability Services (KDADS), Behavioral Health Services Commission
funding_chain: KDADS Problem Gambling and Addictions Fund → contract to DCCCA / Kansas Prevention Collaborative → It Matters KS and statewide problem-gambling toolkits
blurb: The Kansas Prevention Collaborative (KPC) is the KDADS-contracted statewide prevention system that houses Kansas's problem-gambling public-awareness work. Under its "It Matters KS" umbrella, KPC distributes free problem-gambling materials, social-media kits, and the Stacked Deck youth curriculum to regional task forces and community coalitions; promotes the 1-800-522-4700 helpline; and supports Problem Gambling Awareness Month proclamations from the governor. KDADS funded the 2025 Kansas Problem Gambling Prevalence Study to direct future campaign priorities, and the KPC website is the public-facing home for problem-gambling prevention resources.

funding_agency: Michigan Gaming Control Board (MGCB)
funding_chain: MGCB State Services Fee Fund → King Media (creative agency) → Don't Regret the Bet campaign
ncpg_awards: yes (Gold Shorty Impact Award 2026; PRNEWS Digital Award 2024; Emmy Award; multiple finalists)
blurb: "Don't Regret the Bet" is the Michigan Gaming Control Board's flagship responsible-gambling campaign, developed with creative agency King Media after Michigan legalized online casino and sports wagering in 2019-2021. Rather than fear-based messaging, the campaign uses humor, empathy, and relatable scenarios to help bettors recognize early warning signs and set limits. MGCB reports more than 166 million campaign impressions, a 289% estimated ROI, and a near-20% decline in Problem Gambling Helpline call volume during the campaign period coupled with a rise in callers progressing to treatment. The campaign won the 2026 Gold Shorty Impact Award (Mental Health), a 2024 PRNEWS Digital Award, and an Emmy.

funding_agency: Michigan Gaming Control Board (MGCB)
funding_chain: MGCB → King Media → Don't Bet on Your Future youth extension of Don't Regret the Bet
blurb: "Don't Bet on Your Future" is MGCB's youth-focused extension of the Don't Regret the Bet platform, launched May 11, 2026 to address underage and youth-onset problem gambling as sports betting and prediction-market exposure climb among Michigan teens. The initiative bundles classroom-ready resources, a high-school booster-club partnership program (launched March 2026), parent and coach toolkits, and digital outreach across platforms where youth gamble. It is funded directly by MGCB through the State Services Fee Fund and coordinated with MDHHS's problem-gambling helpline so referrals route to in-state treatment providers.

funding_agency: Minnesota Department of Human Services, Behavioral Health Division (Problem Gambling Program)
funding_chain: MN DHS Behavioral Health Division → state contract to Minnesota Alliance on Problem Gambling (MNAPG, formerly Northstar Problem Gambling Alliance) → Let's Talk About It campaign
blurb: "Let's Talk About It" is the Minnesota Alliance on Problem Gambling's statewide public-awareness campaign, re-released across Minnesota in 2026 for Problem Gambling Awareness Month and continued through the year. The signature creative depicts an elephant wrecking an American family room, anchoring the message that gambling harm is the proverbial "elephant in the room" that families and providers need to discuss. MNAPG (the rebranded Northstar Problem Gambling Alliance) is funded through Minnesota DHS Behavioral Health Division contracts and Minnesota Lottery sponsorship, and the campaign supports Problem Gambling Screening Day outreach to behavioral- and primary-care providers. State estimates referenced in the 2026 campaign place 250,000 Minnesota adults and 7,000 high-schoolers at risk.

funding_agency: Missouri Department of Mental Health, Division of Behavioral Health
funding_chain: Missouri Compulsive Gamblers Fund → DMH Division of Behavioral Health → Missouri Alliance to Curb Problem Gambling (DMH + Missouri Gaming Commission + Missouri Lottery + Missouri Gaming Association + Port KC) → 1-888-BETS OFF campaign
blurb: 1-888-BETS OFF is Missouri's statewide problem-gambling public-awareness brand, run by the Missouri Alliance to Curb Problem Gambling — a multi-agency partnership of the Missouri Department of Mental Health's Division of Behavioral Health, the Missouri Gaming Commission, the Missouri Lottery, the Missouri Gaming Association, and the Port Authority of Kansas City. The alliance operates the 1-888-238-7633 helpline, the 888betsoff.org resource hub, and PSAs (television, radio, casino-floor signage) directing Missourians to free counselor referrals; activity expanded significantly with the December 2025 launch of Missouri retail and mobile sports betting, which dedicated a portion of revenue to the Compulsive Gambling Prevention Fund.

funding_agency: Nebraska Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Behavioral Health (formerly Nebraska Commission on Problem Gambling)
funding_chain: Nebraska Charitable Gaming and Gambling Assistance Funds → (through 2025) Nebraska Commission on Problem Gambling; (FY26 transition) NE DHHS Behavioral Health Division → CHOICES Treatment Services and contracted providers → statewide awareness campaign
blurb: problemgambling.nebraska.gov is Nebraska's state-funded problem-gambling public-awareness hub, anchored by the 24/7 Nebraska Confidential Gambling Helpline at 1-833-238-6837. Through 2025 the campaign was administered by the Nebraska Commission on Problem Gambling, which produced television and digital PSAs, Problem Gambling Awareness Month materials, and provider directories. Under a 2026 budget reorganization, the Commission's functions and a reduced appropriation (from roughly $1.5M to $0.9M) are being transferred to the Nebraska DHHS Division of Behavioral Health, which will continue operating the helpline brand and website.

funding_agency: North Dakota Health and Human Services, Behavioral Health Division
funding_chain: Compulsive Gambling Prevention and Treatment Fund → NDHHS Behavioral Health Division → GamblerND public-awareness site and contracted clinicians
blurb: GamblerND is North Dakota HHS's dedicated problem-gambling public-awareness website and campaign, funded through the Compulsive Gambling Prevention and Treatment Fund (capitalized by transfers from the State Lottery and Charitable Gaming Acts). The site hosts a free confidential online screening, personal recovery stories, financial-harm reduction tips, responsible-gambling guidance, and warm hand-offs to NDHHS-contracted outpatient clinicians statewide. Activity peaks each March, when the governor proclaims Problem Gambling Awareness Month and NDHHS amplifies the GamblerND screening tool and provider directory through paid and earned media.

funding_agency: Ohio Department of Mental Health and Addiction Services (OhioMHAS)
funding_chain: OhioMHAS → Prevention Action Alliance (PAA, state contractor) → Origo Branding (creative vendor) → Change The Game Ohio
ncpg_awards: yes (NCPG Public Awareness Campaign award; multiple NCPG honors 2023-2025)
blurb: Change The Game Ohio is Ohio's youth-focused gambling-prevention campaign, funded by OhioMHAS through a contract with Prevention Action Alliance and produced with Origo Branding. It centers on equipping middle- and high-school youth and the parents, educators, and coaches around them with developmentally appropriate messaging about gambling risk, including loot boxes, sports betting, and prediction markets. The campaign maintains classroom toolkits, parent guides, and a public-facing site at changethegameohio.org and has received NCPG Public Awareness Campaign recognition as part of the broader Ohio for Responsible Gambling award sweep in 2023, 2024, and 2025.

campaign_name: Before You Bet (Pause Before You Play)
funding_agency: Ohio for Responsible Gambling (OCCC + Ohio Lottery + Ohio State Racing Commission + OhioMHAS)
funding_chain: Ohio for Responsible Gambling four-agency coalition → Origo Branding (creative vendor) → Before You Bet / Pause Before You Play campaign
ncpg_awards: yes (NCPG Corporate Public Awareness Award 2023; NCPG Media Award 2023; NCPG Corporate People's Choice 2023; additional honors 2024-2025)
blurb: Before You Bet — fronted by the "Pause Before You Play" tagline at pausebeforeyouplay.org — is Ohio for Responsible Gambling's decision-point public-awareness campaign aimed at sports bettors and adult casino and iLottery users. The campaign is funded by the four-agency Ohio for Responsible Gambling coalition (Ohio Casino Control Commission, Ohio Lottery, Ohio State Racing Commission, and OhioMHAS) and produced with Origo Branding. Messaging encourages bettors to pause, set limits, and recognize risk indicators before placing their first bet, and it routes users to the Ohio Problem Gambling Helpline (1-800-589-9966 / 1-800-GAMBLER). Before You Bet has earned multiple NCPG awards across the 2023-2025 conferences.

funding_agency: Ohio for Responsible Gambling (lead: Ohio Casino Control Commission)
funding_chain: Ohio Casino Control Commission (with Ohio Lottery, Racing Commission, OhioMHAS through ORG) → Origo Branding → More Than A Bet campaign
blurb: More Than A Bet is Ohio's student-athlete-focused public-awareness campaign, launched September 2024 by Ohio for Responsible Gambling with the Ohio Casino Control Commission as lead agency. The campaign responds to the NCAA's finding that one in three high-profile athletes receives abusive messages from bettors and that 15-25% of athlete abuse is betting-related. morethanabet.org provides resources for coaches, athletic departments, campus counselors, and athletes themselves, supports anonymous harassment reporting, and offers telehealth-counseling pathways. It complements OCCC's NCAA prop-bet ban (effective March 2024) and was recognized at the 2025 NCPG National Conference.

funding_agency: South Dakota Lottery (Department of Revenue) with South Dakota Department of Social Services, Division of Behavioral Health
funding_chain: SD Lottery responsible-play budget + DSS Behavioral Health Division contract to treatment providers → Keep the Fun Good campaign and 1-888-781-HELP helpline
blurb: "Keep the Fun Good" is the South Dakota Lottery's NASPL- and NCPG-certified responsible-play campaign, run jointly with the SD Department of Social Services Division of Behavioral Health, which administers the state's roughly $170K-$214K annual problem-gambling treatment appropriation funded by lottery transfers. The campaign uses a dedicated webpage, social and broadcast advertising, retailer point-of-sale signage, and Problem Gambling Awareness Month and holiday "Gift Responsibly" activations to remind players to set time and money limits, never chase losses, and call the 1-888-781-HELP problem-gambling helpline. It is the only sustained, state-funded gambling public-awareness brand identified in South Dakota.

funding_agency: Wisconsin Lottery (Department of Revenue) and Wisconsin Department of Health Services (DHS) with Wisconsin Council on Problem Gambling (WCPG, state-contracted)
funding_chain: Wisconsin Lottery transfers and DHS contract → Wisconsin Council on Problem Gambling → 800-GAMBLE-5 helpline, 850-888-HOPE text line, and statewide PGAM campaign
blurb: The Wisconsin Council on Problem Gambling (WCPG) operates Wisconsin's statewide problem-gambling public-awareness brand under contracts with the Wisconsin Lottery (Department of Revenue) and the Wisconsin Department of Health Services. Core deliverables include the 24/7 800-GAMBLE-5 helpline, the 850-888-HOPE text line, the wi-problemgamblers.org resource hub, and the annual Problem Gambling Awareness Month campaign — 2026 carrying the NCPG "Caring Communities, Stronger Futures" theme — co-promoted by the Wisconsin Lottery (which won a NASPL responsible-gaming award in 2025) and the state's tribal-gaming partners. WCPG also runs the annual Wisconsin Problem Gambling Conference, held March 2026 in Waukesha.

No state-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaigns identified for Alaska as of May 2026. Alaska has no NCPG state-council affiliate, no state lottery, no commercial casino, and no licensed sports betting; the Alaska Division of Behavioral Health does not operate a dedicated problem-gambling program. Residents are routed to the NCPG national helpline (1-800-GAMBLER / 1-800-522-4700).

funding_agency: Arizona Department of Gaming, Division of Problem Gambling
funding_chain: Arizona Department of Gaming -> Division of Problem Gambling -> Too Young To Bet AZ
blurb: Too Young To Bet AZ is the state's first youth-focused public-awareness initiative to prevent underage gambling, launched by the Arizona Department of Gaming and its Division of Problem Gambling as part of the National Council on Problem Gambling's broader effort to raise awareness of youth gambling risks. The campaign reinforces that all gambling in Arizona is restricted to age 21+ and equips caregivers, coaches, and educators with toolkits, a college-student guide, and downloadable youth materials. It promotes the statewide 24/7 helpline 1-800-NEXT-STEP (1-800-639-8783) and the text option NEXTSTEP to 53342, both administered by the Division of Problem Gambling under Arizona Department of Gaming oversight.

campaign_name: Arizona Division of Problem Gambling helpline + Take Back the Game PSA
funding_agency: Arizona Department of Gaming, Division of Problem Gambling
funding_chain: Arizona Department of Gaming -> Division of Problem Gambling -> media campaign (Take Back the Game PSA + helpline)
blurb: The Arizona Division of Problem Gambling (DPG) runs the state's central public-awareness portal at problemgambling.az.gov, anchored by the 24/7 helpline 1-800-NEXT-STEP (1-800-639-8783), text NEXTSTEP to 53342, and live chat with master's-level clinicians. DPG's media campaign includes the Take Back the Game digital, TV, and radio PSA promoting the state self-exclusion program at gaming.az.gov/exclude, alongside helpline-highlight ads that drove a 300% increase in helpline contacts after Arizona's 2021 sports-betting launch. The Division is funded through Arizona Department of Gaming appropriations and event-wagering regulatory fees.

campaign_name: Office of Problem Gambling -- 1-800-GAMBLER California Helpline campaign
funding_agency: California Department of Public Health, Office of Problem Gambling (CalOPG)
funding_chain: Tribal Special Distribution Fund -> Gambling Addiction Program Fund -> CDPH Office of Problem Gambling -> problemgambling.ca.gov + California Gambling Education and Treatment Services (CalGETS)
blurb: The California Office of Problem Gambling (CalOPG), housed within the California Department of Public Health, operates problemgambling.ca.gov as the state-funded statewide awareness portal and runs a multi-media outreach campaign targeting problem gamblers across California by age, ethnicity, and geography. CalOPG promotes the 1-800-GAMBLER helpline and text SUPPORT to 53342, with 24/7/365 master's-level counselors in English, Spanish, and Chinese plus telehealth access in 200+ additional languages. The office also administers California Gambling Education and Treatment Services (CalGETS), funded via the tribal Special Distribution Fund and Gambling Addiction Program Fund.

funding_agency: Colorado Department of Revenue, Division of Gaming (under the Colorado Limited Gaming Control Commission)
funding_chain: State gaming-tax appropriations -> Colorado Limited Gaming Control Commission -> Division of Gaming -> Bet Smart Colorado public-education site + Responsible Gaming Grant Program
ncpg_awards: (none documented for the Bet Smart brand itself)
blurb: Bet Smart Colorado is the Colorado Division of Gaming's statewide responsible-gaming public-education campaign, launched during Problem Gambling Awareness Month to promote the Bet Smart principles of setting a budget, treating betting as entertainment, and recognizing when to take a break. The betsmart.colorado.gov hub houses the state's centralized self-exclusion portal launched September 2023, responsible-gaming resources, gray-market gaming guidance, and the Responsible Gaming Grant Program, which has awarded over $7 million since 2023 (including $3.7 million in 2026) to fund prevention, treatment, counselors, public awareness, recovery, and research statewide. The campaign promotes 1-800-GAMBLER as Colorado's helpline number.

campaign_name: You Have the Power to Stop the Show
funding_agency: Problem Gambling Coalition of Colorado (state-funded via Colorado Limited Gaming Control Commission Responsible Gaming Grants)
funding_chain: State gaming-tax appropriations -> Colorado Limited Gaming Control Commission -> Division of Gaming Responsible Gaming Grant Program -> PGCC -> You Have the Power to Stop the Show multi-channel awareness campaign
blurb: You Have the Power to Stop the Show is the Problem Gambling Coalition of Colorado's 2024-launched multi-channel public-awareness campaign that won the National Council on Problem Gambling's 2025 Affiliate Public Awareness Award. Grounded in research-driven strategy and produced with Idea Marketing, the campaign generated more than 15 million digital-platform impressions and reached tens of thousands of Coloradans with the message that those experiencing gambling-related harm have the power, and the resources, to stop. PGCC is funded as a recipient of Colorado Division of Gaming Responsible Gaming Grants (over $1.1 million in early distributions plus subsequent K-12 and collegiate education awards), making the campaign state-agency-funded through the Colorado Limited Gaming Control Commission.

No state-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaigns identified for Hawaii as of May 2026. Hawaii has no legal gambling, no gaming regulator, no dedicated state appropriation for problem gambling, and no NCPG state-council affiliate; the State Department of Health Behavioral Health Administration absorbs limited clinical contact through generalist substance-use services. Residents are routed to the NCPG national helpline at 1-800-GAMBLER (1-800-522-4700).

funding_agency: Idaho State Lottery Commission
funding_chain: Idaho Code Title 67 Ch. 74 lottery appropriation -> Idaho State Lottery Commission -> Play Wise responsible-play initiative
blurb: Play Wise is the Idaho State Lottery's year-round responsible-play public-awareness initiative grounded in a Play Wise philosophy of treating lottery play as entertainment, sticking to an entertainment budget, never borrowing to play, and protecting youth from lottery purchases through retailer training. The Lottery directs Idahoans seeking help to the Idaho Careline (2-1-1) and the National Council on Problem Gambling helpline (1-800-522-4700), and it participates annually in the NCPG/NASPL Gift Responsibly Campaign at Level 2 to raise awareness of underage-gambling risk during the holiday season. There is no separate Idaho Department of Health and Welfare problem-gambling campaign; Idaho has no commercial-casino tax stream to fund a broader effort.

funding_agency: Montana Department of Justice, Gambling Control Division -- partnered with Montana Lottery
funding_chain: State appropriation -> Montana DOJ Gambling Control Division + Montana Lottery (Title 23 Ch. 5 & 7 MCA) -> Keep It Balanced public-information campaign with the Montana Council on Problem Gambling
blurb: Keep It Balanced is the Montana Department of Justice Gambling Control Division and Montana Lottery's joint Problem Gambling Awareness Month public-information campaign reminding Montanans to keep gambling balanced with the rest of their lives. The campaign runs digital advertising at more than 1,100 Montana Lottery retail locations alongside a paid social-media flight, and partners with the Montana Council on Problem Gambling, which operates the 24-hour helpline 1-888-900-9979 and funds outpatient treatment through contracts with more than 20 providers statewide. Helpline operations and treatment contracts are supported through the Montana Department of Public Health and Human Services Behavioral Health Division.

campaign_name: Nevada Council on Problem Gambling -- Problem Gambling Awareness Month / G.A.P.P. campaign
funding_agency: Nevada Department of Health and Human Services, Division of Public and Behavioral Health (DPBH) Problem Gambling Program; Bureau of Behavioral Health, Wellness and Prevention
funding_chain: NRS 458A state Fund for Prevention and Treatment of Problem Gambling -> DHHS Grants Management Unit -> Nevada Council on Problem Gambling -> Problem Gambling Awareness Month + Gambling Awareness Prevention Partnership (G.A.P.P.) sub-grants + Evive app
blurb: The Nevada Council on Problem Gambling, the 1984-founded NCPG state affiliate, runs Nevada's flagship statewide Problem Gambling Awareness Month campaign (2026 theme Caring Communities, Stronger Futures) plus the Gambling Awareness Prevention Partnership (G.A.P.P.) sub-grant program supporting community-based outreach, school presentations, and PGAM events that reached 3,585 Nevadans through 65 events in the first four months of FY26 alone. Funding flows from Nevada Revised Statutes 458A and the State Fund for Prevention and Treatment of Problem Gambling through DHHS DPBH and the DHHS Bureau of Behavioral Health, Wellness and Prevention; the Council promotes 1-800-GAMBLER and the state-grant-funded Evive harm-reduction app.

No state-agency-funded named public-awareness campaign identified for New Mexico as of May 2026. The New Mexico Gaming Control Board maintains a Compulsive & Problem Gambling page and the statewide self-exclusion list but does not run a named state-branded awareness campaign. The Responsible Gaming Association of New Mexico (RGANM, 1-888-696-2440) runs broad public-awareness work and conferences but is operator-funded by tribal and commercial licensees rather than state-appropriation-funded, so it is outside scope under this row's funding criteria.

funding_agency: Oregon State Lottery Commission
funding_chain: ORS 461.549 statutory 1% set-aside of Oregon Lottery net proceeds -> Oregon State Lottery Commission Responsible Gaming program -> Safer Play PSA campaign + Multicultural Communications campaign
ncpg_awards: 2025 NCPG Corporate Public Awareness Award (Safer Play PSA); 2025 NCPG Corporate Multicultural Communications Award; 2025 NCPG Corporate Social Responsibility Award; 2025 Don Feeney Award (Stacy Shaw)
blurb: Safer Play is the Oregon Lottery's statewide responsible-gaming public-awareness PSA and resource campaign that won the National Council on Problem Gambling's 2025 Corporate Public Awareness Award alongside three other 2025 NCPG honors. The campaign gives players tools and resources to make informed choices about Lottery, video lottery terminal, and sports-betting play, with paid media reaching Oregonians statewide. It is funded by the statutory 1% set-aside of Oregon Lottery net proceeds dedicated to problem-gambling services under ORS 461.549 (in place since 1992), which has delivered more than $148 million to prevention and treatment programs to date.

funding_agency: Oregon Health Authority, Problem Gambling Services (with Oregon Lottery and Oregon Council on Problem Gambling)
funding_chain: ORS 461.549 1% Lottery set-aside -> Oregon Health Authority Problem Gambling Services -> OPGR helpline portal (1-877-MYLIMIT / Spanish 1-844-TU-VALES) operated with Oregon Council on Problem Gambling
ncpg_awards: (see Safer Play row for related Oregon Lottery NCPG awards)
blurb: The Oregon Problem Gambling Resource (OPGR) at opgr.org is Oregon's statewide problem-gambling awareness, screening, and treatment-access platform. It is operated as a collaboration between the Oregon Health Authority Problem Gambling Services program, the Oregon Lottery, local community treatment providers, and the Oregon Council on Problem Gambling. The site hosts an in-browser nine-item Problem Gambling Severity Index self-evaluation and routes Oregonians to free counseling via 1-877-MYLIMIT (call/text/chat) or 1-844-TU-VALES for Spanish-speaking callers, 24/7. Funding flows through the ORS 461.549 statutory 1% set-aside of Oregon Lottery net proceeds, which provides approximately $7.5 million annually to problem-gambling prevention and treatment.

funding_agency: Oregon Health Authority, Problem Gambling Services
funding_chain: ORS 461.549 1% Lottery set-aside -> Oregon Health Authority Problem Gambling Services -> Reflect, Resource, Renew county-level prevention framework
blurb: Reflect, Resource, Renew is the Oregon Health Authority Problem Gambling Services program's statewide prevention brand and visual identity, deployed by county behavioral-health authorities (e.g., Jackson County, Josephine County) and prevention providers to deliver community-level problem-gambling prevention. The framework's three pillars are Reflect (help Oregonians reflect on their gambling knowledge and behaviors), Resource (provide responsible-gambling guidelines and signs/symptoms of problem gambling), and Renew (problem-gambling treatment is available and renewal of life and relationships is possible). It is funded through the same ORS 461.549 1% Lottery set-aside that supports OPGR and the statewide treatment network.

No state-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaigns identified for Utah as of May 2026. Utah has a constitutional prohibition on all gambling (Article VI, Section 27), no gambling tax revenue, no dedicated problem-gambling appropriation, and no NCPG state-council affiliate. The Utah Division of Substance Use and Mental Health within the Department of Health and Human Services delivers gambling-related clinical contact through generalist substance-use services; residents are routed to the NCPG national helpline at 1-800-GAMBLER (1-800-522-4700).

campaign_name: Problem Gambling Awareness Month (WSGC + Washington's Lottery + HCA + ECPG)
funding_agency: Washington State Health Care Authority, Division of Behavioral Health and Recovery -- partnered with Washington State Gambling Commission and Washington's Lottery
funding_chain: State appropriation (RCW 9.46.071) -> Washington State Health Care Authority Problem Gambling Program -> contract with Evergreen Council on Problem Gambling (1-800-547-6133 helpline) + WSGC and Washington's Lottery co-promotion
ncpg_awards: (none documented for the joint state-agency PGAM campaign)
blurb: Washington's statewide Problem Gambling Awareness Month campaign is a joint effort of the Washington State Gambling Commission, Washington's Lottery, and the Washington State Health Care Authority (HCA), executed in partnership with the Evergreen Council on Problem Gambling (ECPG). HCA provides direct contracted funding to ECPG for the annual PGAM campaign and for the 24/7 helpline at 1-800-547-6133 (call/text/chat), under the statutory framework of RCW 9.46.071. Recent PGAM campaign components have included the Connections radio and PSA flight produced with KING5, KIRO, KOMO, and Entercom partners, plus a sustained military-and-veterans awareness flight reaching active-duty service members, veterans, and their families.

funding_agency: Washington State Health Care Authority, Division of Behavioral Health and Recovery (Problem Gambling Program contract with ECPG)
funding_chain: State appropriation (RCW 9.46.071) -> Washington State HCA Problem Gambling Program -> Evergreen Council on Problem Gambling -> Take Back Control youth media campaign
blurb: Take Back Control is the Evergreen Council on Problem Gambling's youth-focused media campaign teaching teens and young adults to recognize when gambling mechanics -- loot boxes, social-casino apps, in-game wagering, and microtransactions -- are built into the games and apps they use every day. The campaign is delivered under ECPG's state-funded contract with the Washington State Health Care Authority Problem Gambling Program (RCW 9.46.071), in collaboration with the Recovery Cafe Network and several Washington tribal nations, and routes families to the 24/7 helpline at 1-800-547-6133 (call/text/chat).

campaign_name: Wyoming Gaming Commission Responsible Gaming (PGAM Seeking Understanding)
funding_agency: Wyoming Gaming Commission
funding_chain: Online sports-wagering tax + lottery transfers -> Wyoming Gaming Commission Responsible Gaming program -> Problem Gambling Awareness Month Seeking Understanding campaign + Gambling Disorder Screening Day + self-exclusion promotion via NVSEP
blurb: The Wyoming Gaming Commission's Responsible Gaming program is the state regulator's public-awareness campaign covering online sports wagering, pari-mutuel and historic horse racing, and skill-based amusement games. Each March the Commission runs a Problem Gambling Awareness Month flight (2025 theme Seeking Understanding) of social-media posts, partner emails, and Gambling Disorder Screening Day (March 11) outreach, alongside year-round PSAs and the NVSEP-routed statewide self-exclusion program at gaming.wyo.gov/resources/responsible-gaming/self-exclusion. The Commission lists the National Problem Gambling Helpline (1-800-522-4700), the Wyoming Council on Problem Gambling (wyomingcpg.org), and the Wyoming Department of Health line (1-866-571-0944) as primary referral pathways.

funding_agency: Oklahoma Association on Problem Gambling and Gaming (OAPGG)
funding_chain: ODMHSAS + tribal compact contributions -> OAPGG (NCPG state affiliate) + Oklahoma Indian Gaming Association (OIGA) -> Smart Play OK
ncpg_awards: NCPG Affiliate Public Awareness Award (2020) for Smart Play OK
blurb: Smart Play OK is the statewide responsible-gambling consumer brand launched in November 2019 by the Oklahoma Association on Problem Gambling and Gaming (OAPGG) in partnership with the Oklahoma Indian Gaming Association (OIGA) and a coalition of tribal and casino employees. The campaign unites the state's 38 federally recognized tribes operating more than 130 Class II and Class III casinos under a common responsible-gambling platform, distributing brochures, posters, billboards, and casino-employee training materials, and serving as the public face of Oklahoma's statewide self-exclusion program. OAPGG is funded in part by the Oklahoma Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services (ODMHSAS) under the state's Compulsive Gambling Treatment Program authority at 3A O.S. Sec. 729, with additional support from tribal compact contributions and industry partners. Smart Play OK earned NCPG's Affiliate Public Awareness Award in 2020 and continues to anchor Oklahoma's prevention messaging alongside OAPGG's flagship oapgg.org hub, the statewide self-exclusion portal, and the free Evive mobile app distributed statewide to anyone who wants to play safer, cut back, or stop gambling.

campaign_name: OAPGG Statewide Hub and Evive App Rollout
funding_agency: Oklahoma Association on Problem Gambling and Gaming (OAPGG)
funding_chain: ODMHSAS + tribal compact contributions + industry partners -> OAPGG -> oapgg.org + Evive statewide deployment
ncpg_awards: None identified specific to oapgg.org; OAPGG holds 2020 NCPG award for Smart Play OK
blurb: The OAPGG website at oapgg.org is the state's central consumer-facing problem-gambling hub, distinct from Smart Play OK in that it consolidates self-assessment tools, the statewide self-exclusion enrollment workflow with online notarization, a weekly women's online support group ("Recover Her"), the certified-counselor directory, and free statewide access to the Evive digital therapeutic app. OAPGG was the first NCPG state affiliate to offer Evive statewide, with all certified gambling counselors trained to integrate the app into one-on-one counseling. Funding flows from ODMHSAS (the Oklahoma Department of Mental Health and Substance Abuse Services Problem Gambling Services line), tribal compact set-asides, and private contributions; OAPGG promotes the National Problem Gambling Helpline (1-800-MY-RESET / 1-800-522-4700) with Oklahoma routing. The hub serves general public, clinicians, tribal partners, and family members.

No state/territory-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaigns identified for Texas as of May 2026. Texas is one of seven U.S. states with no dedicated public funding for problem-gambling prevention or treatment; the Texas Health and Human Services Commission does not currently contract a statewide problem-gambling awareness campaign. The Texas Coalition on Problem Gambling (TXCPG, txcpg.org), which became an NCPG state affiliate in April 2025, runs Problem Gambling Awareness Month outreach under the 2026 national theme "Caring Communities, Stronger Futures" but is funded by private sponsors (Las Vegas Sands, DraftKings, Linda Uphoff) rather than the State of Texas, so it falls outside this CSV's state-funded scope. Texans calling 1-800-GAMBLER / 1-800-MY-RESET (1-800-697-3738) are routed to the National Problem Gambling Helpline operated by NCPG. If a future Texas constitutional amendment legalizes commercial casinos or statewide sports betting, every pending bill from 2021-2025 has included a problem-gambling funding mechanism that would presumably support a state-funded campaign.

campaign_name: DC Lottery / OLG Responsible Gaming Program
funding_agency: DC Office of Lottery and Gaming (OLG)
funding_chain: DC Office of the Chief Financial Officer -> Office of Lottery and Gaming (OLG) -> Responsible Gaming Program (WLA Level 2 certified through 2029)
ncpg_awards: WLA Level 2 Responsible Gaming Certification (reaccredited through 2029); DC Lottery participates in NCPG's annual Gift Responsibly / Too Young to Bet Campaign
blurb: The DC Lottery / Office of Lottery and Gaming Responsible Gaming Program is the District's principal state-funded gambling-awareness platform, administered by OLG (an executive-branch unit under the Office of the Chief Financial Officer) under authority of D.C. Code Sec. 36-621.02 and the Sports Wagering Lottery Amendment Act of 2018. The program covers DC Lottery, iLottery, retail and mobile sports wagering, and charitable games, providing the unified self-exclusion registry (18-month, 3-year, 5-year, and lifetime tiers), the problem-gambling-resources page, retailer and licensee training, and year-round messaging promoted heavily during March Problem Gambling Awareness Month. OLG has been reaccredited by the World Lottery Association at Level 2 Responsible Gaming certification through 2029 and participates in NCPG's annual Gift Responsibly / Too Young to Bet holiday campaign with the message "Lottery tickets aren't child's play." The program's three-pillar approach (education, regulatory compliance, and research) is documented in OLG's FY2024 Responsible Gambling Plan. Clinical referrals are coordinated with the DC Department of Behavioral Health.

No state/territory-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaigns identified for American Samoa as of May 2026. American Samoa is a comprehensive prohibition jurisdiction under A.S.C.A. Sec. 46.4301 with no government lottery, no commercial casino, no parimutuel racing, no licensed sports-wagering market, no slot or video-gambling devices, and no commercial gaming regulatory agency; the only legal exception is occasional charitable bingo and raffles under A.S.C.A. Sec. 46.4302. Because there is no legal commercial gambling industry, there is no statutory predicate, funding stream, or regulator for a state-funded awareness campaign. Residents experiencing gambling-related distress can access behavioral-health services through the American Samoa Department of Health and the National Problem Gambling Helpline at 1-800-MY-RESET (1-800-697-3738) operated by NCPG.

No state/territory-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaigns identified for Guam as of May 2026. Guam voters have rejected casino legalization in every referendum it has reached (1977, 1996, 2004, 2008 slots-at-the-track, and 2012 Proposal A), and the territory has no government lottery, no operating racetrack, and no licensed sports-wagering market; the only legal forms are charitable bingo and raffles licensed by the Department of Revenue and Taxation Regulatory Division. With no commercial gaming regulator and no statutory funding stream, no Guam government agency operates a problem-gambling public-awareness campaign. The Guam Behavioral Health and Wellness Center (GBHWC) within the Department of Public Health and Social Services provides general behavioral-health services that can address gambling-related concerns, and the National Problem Gambling Helpline at 1-800-MY-RESET (1-800-697-3738) is available to Guam residents.

No active state/territory-agency-funded gambling-related public-awareness campaigns identified for the CNMI as of May 2026. The Saipan exclusive casino license held by Imperial Pacific International has been suspended since May 2021; the Tinian Dynasty casino closed in the 2010s; and Executive Order 2025-002 abolished the Commonwealth Casino Commission and transferred its authority to the Commonwealth Lottery Commission. Responsible-gaming requirements survive in administrative code (NMIAC Title 175-10.1 for Saipan, NMIAC Title 170-30 for Tinian, and NMIAC Title 70-50.1 for the CNMI Lottery), but with no operating major casino and no published public-facing CLC awareness campaign, the territory's problem-gambling infrastructure is effectively dormant. Behavioral-health services are delivered through the CNMI Commonwealth Healthcare Corporation (CHCC) at chcc.health, and the National Problem Gambling Helpline at 1-800-MY-RESET (1-800-697-3738) is available to residents.

campaign_name: Juega Responsable - Tu mejor jugada tiene limites
funding_agency: Comision de Juegos del Gobierno de Puerto Rico (Gaming Commission)
funding_chain: Act 81-2019 -> Comision de Juegos del Gobierno de Puerto Rico (Gaming Commission) -> Juega Responsable portal (clinical referrals to ASSMCA PAJC via Linea PAS 1-800-981-0023)
blurb: Juega Responsable is the Puerto Rico Gaming Commission's Spanish-language responsible-gambling consumer campaign, fronted by the tagline "Tu mejor jugada tiene limites" (your best play has limits) and hosted on the official .pr.gov Gaming Commission portal. The portal covers all licensed forms regulated by the Commission under Act 81-2019, including casinos, slot routes, mobile and retail sports wagering, horse racing, and daily fantasy contests; it provides a self-assessment via Microsoft Forms, the voluntary self-exclusion application, downloadable Mitos vs. Realidades (Myths vs. Realities) PDF cards, a frequent-players survey, video PSAs (Capsulas Informativas), and operator-side requirements such as time and spend controls and staff training. Clinical referrals are routed to the Linea PAS at 1-800-981-0023 (988 ext.) and to ASSMCA's Programa de Ayuda al Jugador Compulsivo (PAJC), the territory's Compulsive Gamblers Assistance Program established under Act 67 of 1993 with offices statewide. The Gaming Commission also enrolled Puerto Rico in NCPG's National Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program (NVSEP) to extend coverage to participating online operators.

campaign_name: ASSMCA Programa de Ayuda al Jugador Compulsivo (PAJC) / Linea PAS Gambling Track
funding_agency: Administracion de Servicios de Salud Mental y Contra la Adiccion (ASSMCA)
funding_chain: Act 67 of 1993 -> ASSMCA -> Programa de Ayuda al Jugador Compulsivo (PAJC) + Linea PAS 24/7 helpline (1-800-981-0023 / 988 / chat)
blurb: The Programa de Ayuda al Jugador Compulsivo (PAJC) is ASSMCA's free, confidential clinical-treatment and outreach program for Puerto Ricans with gambling-related disorders, established under Act 67 of 1993 and expanded in 2023 to Arecibo and Fajardo in addition to the central San Juan/Bayamon office. PAJC serves people 18 and older affected by casino play, slot machines, horse racing, traditional and electronic lottery, sports betting, scratch cards, bingo, and cards, and provides individual therapy, group therapy, psychoeducation, and family services. The consumer-facing front door is Linea PAS at 1-800-981-0023 (extension 988 routes to the national line), the lineapas.assmca.pr.gov live chat, and the VRS line at 787-615-4112 for Deaf and hard-of-hearing callers. ASSMCA runs an annual social-media awareness push during Problem Gambling Awareness Month each March covering warning signs, prevention, and pathways to care, and the Administrator of ASSMCA holds an ex officio seat on the Puerto Rico Gaming Commission under Act 81-2019, embedding problem-gambling governance directly into licensing decisions.

funding_agency: Virgin Islands Casino Control Commission (VICCC)
funding_chain: 1% of approximately $2.4M annual casino-revenue tax -> VICCC + V.I. Department of Health (MOU Oct 24, 2024 funding training/certification of six counselors) -> PlayWiseVI
blurb: PlayWiseVI is the U.S. Virgin Islands' territory-funded responsible-gambling consumer brand, operated by the Virgin Islands Casino Control Commission (VICCC) at playwisevi.org. The site offers a 10-question problem-gambling self-assessment, an FAQ library, the Voluntary Exclusion request form for the territory's licensed St. Croix casinos (Divi Carina Bay and Caravelle), a community-resources page, and direct routing to the National Problem Gambling Helpline at 1-800-MY-RESET (1-800-697-3738). VICCC funds the program with 1% of the approximately $2.4 million in annual casino gross-revenue taxes paid by licensed operators, and on October 24, 2024 the VICCC and the V.I. Department of Health signed a Memorandum of Understanding under which VICCC funds training and certification of six clinical counselors - three on St. Thomas/St. John and three on St. Croix - to treat gambling disorders. PlayWiseVI promotes the VICCC Voluntary Self-Exclusion Program (1, 2, or 3-year terms with in-person enrollment at 3005 Orange Grove, Christiansted) and links to NCPG, IAGR, ANET, and the American Gaming Association's responsible-gaming resources.
